message:
My mom just showed up to our family dinner with a go-bag in her car. Not because we're evacuating—just because. When I asked what was in it, she rattled off: water, first aid stuff, flashlights, important documents in a folder, snacks. She laughed and called herself 'prepared to the point of ridiculousness.'

But here's the thing? When our power went out for a few hours last month, she had everything we needed without a single trip to the store or moment of "oh no, what do we do?" No stress, no scrambling. Just... handled.

I used to think people like her were overthinking it. Now I get it. Being ready doesn't mean you're expecting disaster—it just means you're not caught off guard by regular life stuff. And honestly? That peace of mind is kind of nice.

Have you got a go-bag situation happening, or is that still on the to-do list?
